manifest.author: Lattice
manifest.description: Exercises the `form` container in BOTH layout modes plus a standalone widget, under the E3-S2 grammar. Each form is a content leaf wrapped in a block; its widget children stay form-internals (not block-wrapped). The first form uses flow mode (compact label+control rows, split into two columns) to gather string, boolean, and enum inputs. The second form uses grid mode (a weighted column/row grid identical to container's) and places each widget into an explicit 1-indexed cell. Alongside the forms, a single standalone widget stands in a variable-box region — no form wrapper — proving widgets are ordinary item instances. A block-wrapped table consumes every variable via $var typed bindings and ${} string templates, so changing any control re-resolves the document and updates the summary live. Conforms to https://lattice.dev/schemas/dashboard/1.0.0.
manifest.title: Form Container Example

constraints.description: When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
Placeholder text shown in the empty control — editable at runtime.
When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
How the option set is ordered for display — editable at runtime.

config.description: Stream new readings as they arrive.
config.label: Live updates

constraints.description: When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.

The form's layout declaration (E3-S3 surface). Editable at runtime: the layout `mode` (flow vs grid) and, in flow mode, the unitless `columns` count children pack into; in grid mode the relative-weight `columns`/`rows` track lists and `gap`. All unitless and relative — NO CSS units or keywords. Declared with the array variable type per the top-level-only surface limitation (the layout is an object whose editable sub-fields are described here, matching the container `grid` surface convention).

columns.description: Flow mode: a unitless integer column count (1–12). Grid mode: a positive-number relative-weight track array.

constraints.description: The block's human heading/label — editable at runtime.
Whether the block (and the content it wraps) is shown — editable at runtime to toggle the block on or off.

config.description: Flag a metric above this value.
config.label: Alert threshold

constraints.description: When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
Placeholder text shown in the empty control — editable at runtime.
How the box arranges its variable widgets — editable at runtime. `stacked` lays them out one per row; `inline` flows them along a single row. Semantic and renderer-agnostic: NO CSS units or keywords.
The table's column definitions (E3-S2 surface). Editable at runtime to control which columns are shown and in what order. Each entry is a column object with a required header label.

The query parameters passed to the table's bound connection (E3-S2 surface). Editable at runtime to re-parameterize the fetch. Requires a connectionId; parameter values may reference variables via $var / ${} bindings, filled by the resolver's interpolation pass before binding.
The heading rendered above the table — editable at runtime.
The block's human heading/label — editable at runtime.
Whether the block (and the content it wraps) is shown — editable at runtime to toggle the block on or off.

The container's relative-weight grid (E3-S2 surface). Editable at runtime: the column track weights, the row track weights, and the inter-track gap — all unitless, relative numbers. Column and row weights are positive-number arrays the resolver normalizes to fractions summing to 1 per axis; gap is a non-negative number in the same relative space. NO CSS units or keywords.

The container's relative-weight grid (E3-S2 surface). Editable at runtime: the column track weights, the row track weights, and the inter-track gap — all unitless, relative numbers. Column and row weights are positive-number arrays the resolver normalizes to fractions summing to 1 per axis; gap is a non-negative number in the same relative space. NO CSS units or keywords.
An optional longer-form description of the dashboard.

How present a separating edge is around the styled surface.
How tightly repeated/listed content is packed.
How prominent the styled element appears.
How softened the corners of the styled surface are.
The relative amount of internal/outer breathing room.
The semantic colour intent, expressed by meaning.
